purpose: Shared infrastructure for the bats-core test suite. Provides two complementary helpers: test_helper.bash bootstraps a clean, isolated test environment (tmpdir, env defaults, lib sourcing, fixtures) and mock_claude.bash installs a PATH-based fake claude binary that intercepts all claude -p calls with scripted responses — enabling deterministic, zero-cost testing of the full multi-phase agent orchestration pipeline.

architecture.overview: Two-file helper layer loaded via bats `load` directives. test_helper.bash is the foundation — it must be loaded first, creates the temp filesystem, exports all env defaults, and sources every lib in dependency order (matching ultrainit.sh's real startup). mock_claude.bash is optional and layered on top; it writes a mock binary to a temp dir and prepends it to $PATH so every claude call in the process tree is intercepted. The two files are independent: unit tests for pure functions only load test_helper; tests exercising run_agent or run_agents_parallel also load mock_claude.

subdirectories.purpose: Flat — no subdirectories. Two files that together define the entire test support layer.

architecture.data_flow: Test setup() calls _common_setup() → creates TEST_TMPDIR/{workdir,target} → exports env defaults → sources all 9 lib files in order → (optionally) calls setup_mock_claude() which writes a mock binary to TEST_TMPDIR/mock_bin/ and prepends it to PATH. During a test, any code calling run_agent/run_agents_parallel invokes the mock binary, which logs CALL:/STDIN: lines to MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG and returns a pre-configured JSON envelope. After the test, _common_teardown() calls rm -rf TEST_TMPDIR to remove everything atomically.

key_files.purpose: Core test bootstrap. Loads bats-support/bats-assert/bats-file, detects PROJECT_ROOT from its own path, defines _common_setup/_common_teardown, source_lib, use_fixture, and make_claude_envelope.

Mock claude binary installer. Defines setup_mock_claude (writes the binary, prepends PATH), create_dispatch_response, and create_dispatch_error. Supports three response modes: single-file, dispatch-by-name, and error simulation.

patterns.name: PATH-shadowing mock binary
patterns.description: Instead of function-level mocking (which fails across subprocesses), mock_claude.bash writes a real executable to TEST_TMPDIR/mock_bin/claude and prepends that dir to $PATH. Every child process — including those spawned by run_agents_parallel — inherits the modified PATH and calls the mock. The mock binary is a heredoc written with cat > file <<'MOCK_SCRIPT' ... MOCK_SCRIPT so single quotes inside it are literal. Call setup_mock_claude() in setup() after _common_setup(), never before (TEST_TMPDIR must exist first).

patterns.name: Environment-baked child scripts for parallel agents
patterns.description: run_agents_parallel() cannot pass variables to background bash processes via shell functions — each agent runs in its own bash -c subshell. Instead, it writes a temporary script per agent that hard-expands all parent variables as literals (export WORK_DIR="$WORK_DIR"), then sources the libs fresh. This prevents re-sourcing config.sh from overwriting computed values. Tests verify this by checking MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG for multiple CALL: lines after run_agents_parallel.

patterns.name: Response envelope wrapping via make_claude_envelope
patterns.description: Every mock response must match the real claude -p --output-format json envelope: {is_error, total_cost_usd, structured_output}. make_claude_envelope() takes a JSON string as $1, cost as $2 (default 0.15), is_error as $3 (default false) and outputs via jq. Use: make_claude_envelope '{"key":"val"}' "0.42" > "$MOCK_CLAUDE_RESPONSE". For budget-exhaustion tests, write the envelope manually with subtype: "error_max_budget_usd".

patterns.name: Dispatch mode for multi-agent tests
patterns.description: Set MOCK_CLAUDE_DISPATCH_DIR before calling setup_mock_claude, then call create_dispatch_response "agent-name" "$json" "cost" for each agent. The mock binary tries two strategies: (1) case-insensitive substring match of the agent name against --json-schema path, (2) case-insensitive substring match against the full prompt text. First match wins. Falls back to MOCK_CLAUDE_RESPONSE if neither matches. Ordering of .json files in the dispatch dir matters due to glob expansion — name files to avoid prefix ambiguity (e.g., docs-scanner.json before docs.json would never match docs.json).

patterns.name: Full lib sourcing in every test
patterns.description: _common_setup() sources all 9 lib files (utils → config → agent → gather → ask → research → synthesize → validate → merge). This matches ultrainit.sh's startup and catches cross-lib bugs (e.g., synthesize.sh enabling nullglob that breaks config.sh glob patterns). Tests that only need one lib still call _common_setup() for isolation, then may call source_lib() for additional targeted sourcing.

patterns.name: Resumability testing via FORCE flag
patterns.description: Pre-create a findings file then assert the mock was NOT called: echo '{"cached":true}' > "$WORK_DIR/findings/test-agent.json"; FORCE=false; run run_agent ...; assert_success; run cat "$MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG"; refute_output --partial "CALL:". Invert with FORCE=true to verify re-run behavior.

patterns.name: Large-prompt stdin routing
patterns.description: run_agent switches from passing the prompt as a CLI arg to piping via stdin when len(prompt) > 100000 bytes (invokes claude -p - instead of claude -p "$prompt"). The mock captures stdin and logs "STDIN: N bytes" to MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG. Tests assert --partial "STDIN:" and --partial " -p -" in the log to verify the switch happened.

patterns.name: Cost file-per-agent accumulation
patterns.description: Each agent writes a cost file at $WORK_DIR/costs/<name>.cost with format "phase|agent_name|cost_usd" (one file per agent, no race conditions from concurrent writes). Budget tests pre-populate these files to simulate prior spending, then assert that subsequent agent calls are rejected.

conventions.naming: Helper functions use snake_case prefixed by domain: _common_setup/_common_teardown (leading underscore = internal convention, not enforced), setup_mock_claude, make_claude_envelope, create_dispatch_response, create_dispatch_error. Environment variables are SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, prefixed MOCK_CLAUDE_ for mock controls and TEST_ for test-local paths. Bats test descriptions are plain English sentences.
conventions.imports: Each .bats file loads helpers with bats `load` directive using relative paths from the test file's location: load '../helpers/test_helper' and load '../helpers/mock_claude'. The load directive omits the .bash extension (bats convention). Helpers themselves use absolute paths via PROJECT_ROOT for all lib and fixture references.
conventions.error_handling: The mock binary uses ${VAR:-default} for all environment variable reads to prevent unbound-variable errors. Tests use assert_failure / assert_success from bats-assert rather than checking $? directly. Budget errors are signaled by run_agent returning exit 1; test code checks assert_failure without needing to inspect the error message unless testing the diagnostic output specifically.

internal: lib/utils.sh — sourced first; provides json_get, json_merge, mark_phase_complete, is_phase_complete
lib/config.sh — sourced second; reads env vars set in _common_setup to configure budgets
lib/agent.sh — sourced third; run_agent and run_agents_parallel are the primary targets of mock_claude
lib/gather.sh, lib/ask.sh, lib/research.sh, lib/synthesize.sh, lib/validate.sh, lib/merge.sh — sourced together to catch cross-lib interaction bugs
tests/fixtures/ — JSON fixture files copied via use_fixture() for realistic agent response data
schemas/ — test code creates minimal {"type":"object"} schemas at TEST_TMPDIR/schema.json

external_key.name: bats-core v1.11.1
external_key.purpose: Test runner — provides @test blocks, run helper, setup/teardown lifecycle

external_key.name: bats-support
external_key.purpose: Base assertion utilities required by bats-assert

external_key.name: bats-file
external_key.purpose: assert_file_exists, assert_file_not_exists, assert_dir_exists for filesystem assertions

external_key.name: jq
external_key.purpose: JSON construction and parsing in make_claude_envelope, create_dispatch_response, create_dispatch_error

external_key.name: bc
external_key.purpose: Floating-point budget arithmetic in lib/config.sh (not in helpers directly, but required for sourced libs to work)

gotchas.issue: setup_mock_claude() must be called AFTER _common_setup() because the mock binary is written to TEST_TMPDIR/mock_bin/ which doesn't exist until _common_setup() runs mktemp -d.
gotchas.solution: Always call _common_setup first, then setup_mock_claude in the same setup() block.

gotchas.issue: Dispatch mode uses case-insensitive substring matching (grep -qi) on both the schema path and the full prompt text. An agent named 'docs' will match before 'docs-scanner' if 'docs.json' appears first in the glob expansion of the dispatch directory.
gotchas.solution: Name dispatch files unambiguously. If two agents share a prefix, ensure the longer/more-specific name appears first alphabetically (e.g., docs-scanner.json beats docs.json lexically and matches its own name first).

gotchas.issue: Env variables must be exported BEFORE sourcing the libs. config.sh uses ${VAR:-default} which reads the variable at source time. If you set TOTAL_BUDGET after sourcing, budget calculations use the old value.
gotchas.solution: Set all environment overrides before calling _common_setup, or re-source lib/config.sh after changing budget variables.

gotchas.issue: The mock binary is written as a heredoc with a quoted delimiter (<<'MOCK_SCRIPT') so variable references inside it like ${MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG} are literals at write time and expand at execution time. However, local inside the heredoc is a bash local keyword that only works inside a function. The mock binary is a top-level script, so local declarations are technically invalid (bash silently ignores the local keyword at global scope, but set -euo pipefail may not surface this).
gotchas.solution: The mock binary does not use set -euo pipefail, so local at global scope is silent. Be aware that variables declared with local in the mock script are actually global — do not rely on scoping for isolation between dispatch iterations.

gotchas.issue: run_agents_parallel cleans up its temp script dir only after all agents finish. If a test kills the process mid-run, orphaned dirs matching /tmp/ultrainit-agents.* accumulate.
gotchas.solution: Edge-case tests assert no orphaned dirs remain after a full run. For manual cleanup: rm -rf /tmp/ultrainit-agents.*

gotchas.issue: make_claude_envelope escapes the default value for structured_output as \{\}: local structured_output="${1:-\{\}}". If you pass an empty string as $1, it uses {} (literal braces), not an empty string — jq then wraps {} as the structured_output object.
gotchas.solution: Always pass a valid JSON object as $1. To test empty responses, pass '{}' explicitly or write a raw JSON file without using make_claude_envelope.

gotchas.issue: The mock captures stdin with STDIN_CONTENT=$(cat) even when no stdin data is piped. In a subshell context without a TTY, [[ ! -t 0 ]] is always true, so the mock always attempts to read stdin — this would hang if data is expected but never sent.
gotchas.solution: The mock only logs STDIN if byte count > 0, so hanging is prevented because cat returns immediately on EOF when the pipe is closed. This is safe as long as the caller closes stdin (which run_agent does via echo pipe).

gotchas.issue: MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG is created by the setup_mock_claude function (: > "$MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG"), but its path is referenced inside the mock binary heredoc as ${MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG:-/dev/null}. If MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG is not exported (just assigned), child processes won't see it and logs go to /dev/null.
gotchas.solution: setup_mock_claude uses export MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG=... which ensures child process inheritance. Never reassign MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG without re-exporting.

domain_terms.meaning: The outer JSON structure {is_error, total_cost_usd, structured_output} that wraps every claude -p --output-format json response. make_claude_envelope() generates these for tests.
Mock routing strategy where MOCK_CLAUDE_DISPATCH_DIR contains one .json file per agent name; the mock binary routes each call to the matching file based on schema path or prompt content.
The structured JSON output files written by each agent to $WORK_DIR/findings/<name>.json. Tests pre-populate these to simulate completed phases or test resumability.
Convention functions called in every bats setup()/teardown() pair to create/destroy the isolated test environment. Leading underscore signals internal/shared status.
Helper that sources a single lib file by relative name from PROJECT_ROOT/lib/. Used when a test needs to force-reload one lib after changing env vars.
Helper that copies a pre-canned JSON file from tests/fixtures/ into a test's working directory, providing realistic agent output without real Claude calls.
Env var controlling resumability. FORCE=false (default in tests) makes run_agent skip agents whose findings files already exist. FORCE=true re-runs and overwrites.
Env var set by run_agent indicating which pipeline phase it belongs to (gather/research/synthesize/validate). Written into cost files to enable per-phase budget accounting.

commands.purpose: Run all unit tests inside Docker using the pre-built test image
Run validate-skill.sh and validate-subagent.sh script tests
Run edge case tests (budget exhaustion, special characters, corrupt state)
Run the full bats suite recursively across all test subdirectories
Build the Dockerfile.test image locally (one-time setup before running tests)
Run only helper-adjacent tests directly (requires bats installed locally)

skill_opportunities.name: add-agent-test
skill_opportunities.description: Scaffold a new bats unit test for a run_agent call: generate the test file with correct load directives, setup/teardown calling _common_setup/_common_teardown, a minimal schema file, a make_claude_envelope response, and at least three @test blocks covering success, skip-on-existing-findings, and budget-exhaustion.
workflow_steps: Identify the agent name and schema file being tested
Create tests/unit/<agent>_run.bats with load directives and setup/teardown
Write @test for success: mock response → assert findings file written, cost recorded
Write @test for resumability: pre-create findings, FORCE=false → assert mock not called
Write @test for budget exhaustion: pre-populate cost files → assert_failure

skill_opportunities.name: add-dispatch-fixture
skill_opportunities.description: Add a new dispatch response fixture for integration tests: create a realistic JSON file in tests/fixtures/findings/<agent>.json and wire it into the relevant integration test's dispatch setup.
workflow_steps: Run the real agent once and capture its structured_output
In the integration test setup(), call create_dispatch_response with the fixture
Verify dispatch matching works by running the test and checking MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG
